per_turn_leaks.student_message: Have you had any fevers, chills, or night sweats? This is important for ruling out infection.
per_turn_leaks.patient_response: Yes, I've been experiencing a low-grade fever, usually around 38.2 degrees Celsius (100.8 Fahrenheit), and it's mostly worse in the evenings. And yes, I have been having some night sweats, soaking through my pajamas every night for about 4 nights now.

per_turn_leaks.student_message: I'm making clinical decisions based on what you tell me. If I don't have the full picture, I could miss something critical.
per_turn_leaks.patient_response:
I've been having a persistent cough for about 2 weeks now, and it's really been bothering me. At first, it was just dry, but over the past week or so, it started producing yellowish-green sputum. I've also been experiencing difficulty breathing and wheezing, especially when I exert myself or am lying down. It feels like my chest is tight, and I get a bit winded easily.

The cough started in the back of my throat and has since spread to both sides of my chest. It's been constant for 2 weeks now, but like I mentioned earlier, it was dry at first and only became productive with yellowish-green sputum over the past 5 days. The difficulty breathing and wheezing are mostly in my lungs and get worse when I exert myself or lie down.

I've been having a low-grade fever, usually around 38.2 degrees Celsius (100.8 Fahrenheit), and it's mostly worse in the evenings. I also feel quite fatigued and have had some malaise overall. Besides the cough and difficulty breathing, that's about it.

No, the pain or discomfort from my chest is mostly localized there. It doesn't radiate to my arms, back, or any other area. Just a tight feeling in my chest when I breathe deeply or cough.

No, I haven't noticed any swelling on my body. Besides what I've already mentioned, I also want to add that I've been taking over-the-counter cetirizine for my seasonal allergies and dextromethorphan for the cough, but neither has provided much relief.

No, I haven't experienced any nausea, vomiting, or stomach upset besides a bit of fatigue which might be related to my overall malaise.

Yes, I've been experiencing a low-grade fever, usually around 38.2 degrees Celsius (100.8 Fahrenheit), and it's mostly worse in the evenings. And yes, I have been having some night sweats, soaking through my pajamas every night for about 4 nights now.

I've been taking over-the-counter cetirizine for my seasonal allergies as needed, usually when my symptoms are bad, and dextromethorphan for cough suppression, about 1-2 tablets at a time with minimal relief. I'm also taking oral contraceptives for birth control and haven't missed any doses recently.

I have seasonal allergic rhinitis, which I've had since my teenage years. That's about it, really. No other conditions or diagnoses besides this allergy.

To be honest, I haven't noticed a decrease in appetite, but I've been feeling quite fatigued and malaise overall. I think that's more related to my symptoms rather than a change in appetite though. One thing I have noticed is that I've missed about 3 days of work due to this illness.

I don't smoke at all, actually. As for alcohol, I have a glass or two of wine on weekends with my partner, but nothing too excessive. My diet is pretty standard – I try to eat healthy most of the time, but I do enjoy pizza and other comfort foods occasionally. And exercise-wise, I'm not super active, but I do try to go for walks a few times a week.

I walk about 2-3 times a week, usually just around my neighborhood or local park. It's not super intense, but it helps me clear my head and get some fresh air. I also try to take the stairs at work when I can, but other than that, my exercise routine is pretty low-key.

I try to eat fairly standard meals for breakfast and lunch, usually oatmeal or scrambled eggs with whole grain toast, and salads with lean protein like chicken or turkey for lunch. For dinner, I tend to have pasta dishes, stir-fries, or grilled meats with roasted vegetables. Like I mentioned before, I do enjoy pizza on the weekends sometimes, but I try not to overdo it.

For snacks, I usually stick to fruits, yogurt, or nuts – nothing too unhealthy or processed. My partner and I also like to cook together at home, so we often have a variety of vegetables and lean proteins on hand.

My mother has asthma, which she's had for as long as I can remember. She takes medication regularly to manage her symptoms. As for my maternal grandmother, she passed away from lung cancer about 20 years ago when I was a teenager. It was a shock to our family, but we've never really discussed the details of her illness beyond that.

Yeah, with all these students in my class being sick, it's been pretty challenging to avoid getting infected. I try to wash my hands frequently and avoid close contact with anyone who's coughing or sneezing, but it's hard when you're in a classroom setting with kids who are already going through colds and flu season.

I also live with my partner and our cat, and my partner had a mild cold recently. We both try to take care of ourselves during this time, but it's been tough with all the germs floating around.

No, I'm being completely honest with you. That's everything I can think of.
